<!--
Thanks for contributing! Please fill this out — it makes review much faster.
See CONTRIBUTING.md for the full style guide if you haven't already.
-->

## What does this PR do?

<!-- e.g. "Adds a new topic on X under category Y" / "Fixes an inaccurate claim in the Z topic" / "Rewords a confusing definition" -->



## Type of change

- [ ] New topic
- [ ] Edit/fix to an existing topic
- [ ] New category
- [ ] App/UI change (HTML/CSS/JS outside the `TOPICS` content)
- [ ] Other (describe above)

## Checklist

- [ ] My topic's `cat` matches an existing category code exactly (case-sensitive), or I've proposed a new category and opened an issue to discuss it first.
- [ ] My `title` doesn't already exist elsewhere in the file.
- [ ] My `def` uses line breaks for multi-part explanations — no dense, run-on lists of sub-concepts crammed into one paragraph.
- [ ] If my topic is an acronym, I spell out what each letter stands for before diving into the explanation.
- [ ] My `ex` is a concrete, ideally fintech-grounded scenario — not an abstract "object A and object B" example.
- [ ] My `trade` array includes genuine downsides or "when NOT to use this," not just a list of benefits.
- [ ] I opened `index.html` in a browser and confirmed my topic renders correctly, shows up in search, and nothing else broke.
- [ ] No stray backtick characters (`` ` ``) inside any of my template literal strings.
- [ ] If I added a `code` field, it's short, illustrative (not production-grade), and has comments explaining *why*, not just *what*.

## Anything you're unsure about?

<!-- If you're not 100% sure a technical detail is accurate, say so here — a reviewer can double-check rather than you guessing confidently. Totally fine to flag this. -->
